from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    predicate_templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway number of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway number of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the ICAO identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the IATA identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is natively known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by the {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"the official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"the largest city of {s} is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"the county seat of {s} is {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"the mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the city of {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda s, o: f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates the {o} helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"the currency of {s} is the {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as its hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates the {o} fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"the chief of {s} is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_predicates = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_predicates[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Find the main subject (likely the airport)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    # Try to find airport subject first
    for subj in subject_predicates:
        if "airport" in subj.lower() or "Airport" in subj:
            main_subject = subj
            break

    parts = []
    used_subjects = set()

    def process_triple(triple):
        pred = triple.predicate
        subj = triple.subject
        obj = triple.object
        if pred in predicate_templates:
            return predicate_templates[pred](subj, obj)
        else:
            return f"the {pred} of {subj} is {obj}"

    # Build sentence from all triples
    sentences = []
    for triple in triples:
        sentences.append(process_triple(triple))

    if len(sentences) == 1:
        return sentences[0].capitalize() + "."
    elif len(sentences) == 2:
        return sentences[0].capitalize() + " and " + sentences[1] + "."
    else:
        result = sentences[0].capitalize()
        for i, sent in enumerate(sentences[1:], 1):
            if i == len(sentences) - 1:
                result += ", and " + sent
            else:
                result += ", " + sent
        return result + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

            elif t.predicate == "order":

        runway_desc = f"the {runway_num} runway" if runway_num else "the runway"

        classification_parts = []

        if order_val:

        sentence = f"{surface_type_obj}"
        if division_val:

        if classification_parts:

        sentence += f", and is the surface type of {runway_desc} of {airport_subject}."
        return sentence

    def process_triple_preserve_case(triple):
        pred = triple.predicate
        subj = triple.subject
        obj = triple.object

        else:

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    from collections import defaultdict

    predicate_templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda s, o: f"has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",

        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"has a first runway number of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"has a fifth runway number of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"has the ICAO identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"has the IATA identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"has the location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"is natively known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"is led by the {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"uses the {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"the official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"is led by {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"the largest city of {s} is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"the county seat of {s} is {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda s, o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"the mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"has a runway surface of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"is in the city of {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda s, o: f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda s, o: f"operates the {o} helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": lambda s, o: f"uses {o} as a transport aircraft",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"the currency of {s} is the {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"belongs to the division {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"belongs to the order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"uses {o} as its hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda s, o: f"operates the {o} fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": lambda s, o: f"uses the {o} as an attack aircraft",
        "battle": lambda s, o: f"participated in the {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"the chief of {s} is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"has the postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"has the area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda s, o: f"is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    # Standalone predicates that form their own sentence with full subject
    standalone_predicates = {
        "capital", "officialLanguage", "largestCity", "countySeat",
        "mayor", "currency", "chief", "leaderTitle", "demonym"
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_groups[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Determine main subject priority: prefer airport, then first triple's subject
    def subject_priority(subj):
        sl = subj.lower()
        if "airport" in sl:
            return 0
        return 1

    subjects_ordered = sorted(subject_groups.keys(), key=subject_priority)
    main_subject = subjects_ordered[0]

    # Build sentence parts grouped by subject
    sentence_parts = []

    for subj in subjects_ordered:
        group = subject_groups[subj]
        subj_phrases = []
        standalone_phrases = []

        for triple in group:
            pred = triple.predicate
            obj = triple.object
            if pred in predicate_templates:
                phrase = predicate_templates[pred](subj, obj)
            else:
                phrase = f"the {pred} of {subj} is {obj}"

            # Check if this predicate needs standalone sentence
            if pred in standalone_predicates:
                standalone_phrases.append(phrase)
            else:
                # Check if template already includes subject
                if phrase.startswith(subj):
                    standalone_phrases.append(phrase)
                else:
                    subj_phrases.append(phrase)

        if subj_phrases:
            # Combine subject-relative phrases
            if len(subj_phrases) == 1:
                combined = f"{subj} {subj_phrases[0]}"
            elif len(subj_phrases) == 2:
                combined = f"{subj} {subj_phrases[0]} and {subj_phrases[1]}"
            else:
                combined = f"{subj} {', '.join(subj_phrases[:-1])}, and {subj_phrases[-1]}"
            sentence_parts.append(combined)

        for sp in standalone_phrases:
            sentence_parts.append(sp)

    if not sentence_parts:
        return ""

    # Capitalize first letter of the whole sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        result = sentence_parts[0]
    elif len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        result = sentence_parts[0] + ", and " + sentence_parts[1]
    else:
        result = ", ".join(sentence_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + sentence_parts[-1]

    result = result[0].upper() + result[1:]
    if not result.endswith("."):
        result += "."
    return result

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: cityServed - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | cityServed | "Aarhus, Denmark")
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevel - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevel | 25.0)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | location | Tirstrup)
Predicate: operatingOrganisation - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | operatingOrganisation | "Aarhus Lufthavn A/S")
Predicate: runwayLength - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayLength | 2776.0)
Predicate: runwayName - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayName | "10L/28R")
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | country | United States)

        return 1

    subjects_ordered = sorted(subject_groups.keys(), key=subject_priority)
    main_subject = subjects_ordered[0]

    # Build sentence parts grouped by subject

            elif len(subj_phrases) == 2:

            else:
                combined = f"{subj} {', '.join(subj_phrases[:-1])}, and {subj_phrases[-1]}"
            sentence_parts.append(combined)

        for sp in standalone_phrases:
            sentence_parts.append(sp)

    if not sentence_parts:
        return ""

    # Capitalize first letter of the whole sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        result = sentence_parts[0]
    elif len(sentence_parts) == 2:
        result = sentence_parts[0] + ", and " + sentence_parts[1]
    else:

    result = result[0].upper() + result[1:]
    if not result.endswith("."):

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    predicate_templates = {
        "cityServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda s, o: f"the runway length of {s} is {o}",
        "runwayName": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway named {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a runway surface type of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway surface type of {o}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a first runway numbered {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a second runway surface type of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a third runway surface type of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fourth runway surface type of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway numbered {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a fifth runway surface type of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the ICAO location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the IATA location identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the location identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is natively known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by the {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{o} is spoken in {s}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"the official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"the leader of {s} is {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda s, o: f"the largest city of {s} is {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda s, o: f"the county seat of {s} is {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the administrative arrondissement of {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"the mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda s, o: f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"the inhabitants of {s} are known as {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} helicopters",
        "transportAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as transport aircraft",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"the currency of {s} is {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda s, o: f"{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the class {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the division {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda s, o: f"{s} has its hub at {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates {o} fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o} as attack aircraft",
        "battle": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o}",
        "chief": lambda s, o: f"the chief of {s} is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda s, o: f"{s} has the area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_triples = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_triples[triple.subject].append(triple)

    # Find primary subject (airport if possible)
    primary_subject = triples[0].subject
    for subj in subject_triples:
        if "Airport" in subj or "airport" in subj:
            primary_subject = subj
            break

    parts = []
    used = set()

    # Process primary subject first
    for triple in subject_triples[primary_subject]:
        pred = triple.predicate
        obj = triple.object
        if pred in predicate_templates:
            parts.append(predicate_templates[pred](triple.subject, obj))
        else:
            parts.append(f"{triple.subject} has {pred} of {obj}")
        used.add(id(triple))

    # Process remaining subjects
    for subj, subj_triples in subject_triples.items():
        if subj == primary_subject:
            continue
        for triple in subj_triples:
            if id(triple) in used:
                continue
            pred = triple.predicate
            obj = triple.object
            if pred in predicate_templates:
                parts.append(predicate_templates[pred](triple.subject, obj))
            else:
                parts.append(f"{triple.subject} has {pred} of {obj}")
            used.add(id(triple))

    if not parts:
        t = triples[0]
        return f"The {t.predicate} of {t.subject} is {t.object}."

    if len(parts) == 1:
        return parts[0] + "."

    # Join parts into a coherent sentence without lowercasing entity names
    sentence = parts[0]
    for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], start=1):
        sentence += ", where " + part if i == 1 else ", and " + part
    sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: cityServed - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | cityServed | "Aarhus, Denmark")
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevel - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevel | 25.0)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | location | Tirstrup)
Predicate: operatingOrganisation - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | operatingOrganisation | "Aarhus Lufthavn A/S")
Predicate: runwayLength - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayLength | 2776.0)
Predicate: runwayName - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayName | "10L/28R")
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | country | United States)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | isPartOf | Jones County, Texas)
Predicate: 1stRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 1stRunwayLengthFeet | 3678)
Predicate: 1stRunwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 1stRunwaySurfaceType | Asphalt)
Predicate: 3rdRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 3rdRunwayLengthFeet | 7202)
Predicate: icaoLocationIdentifier - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | icaoLocationIdentifier | "KABI")

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      from collections import defaultdict
  
      by_subject = defaultdict(list)
      for t in triples:
          by_subject[t.subject].append(t)
  
      primary_subject = triples[0].subject
      for s in by_subject:
          if "airport" in s.lower():
              primary_subject = s
              break
  
      def clean_language(x: str) -> str:
          if x.lower().endswith(" language"):
              return x[:-9]
          return x
  
      def short_subject_for_country(s: str, country_obj: str) -> str:
          if ", " in s:
              left, right = s.rsplit(", ", 1)
              if right.strip().lower() == country_obj.strip().lower():
                  return left.strip()
          return s
